summary: Matched two previously missing fighter common air scope item enter functions in ftCo_Attack100.c: fn_800D7C60 for ItemScopeAirRapid and fn_800D81D0 for ItemScopeAirFire. Both implementations mirror nearby ground/item-scope counterparts while adding the air-specific ftCommon_ClampAirDrift call after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

key_files.change: Added matched C implementations for fn_800D7C60 and fn_800D81D0.

what_changed: Added fn_800D7C60(Fighter_GObj* gobj), identified in the PR text as the 140-byte air scope rapid enter function and the air version of fn_800D7BDC.

fn_800D7C60 computes a motion state with fn_800D769C(fp, ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirRapid), calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with flag 0x02000000 and ftCo_804D9028/ftCo_804D902C float constants, clamps air drift, initializes fp->mv.co.common.x0 from p_ftCommonData + 0x5BC, and installs accessory4_cb/take_dmg_cb.
Added fn_800D81D0(Fighter_GObj* gobj, int arg1), identified in the PR text as the 152-byte air scope fire enter function and the air version of fn_800D8140.

fn_800D81D0 clears fp->throw_flags, computes a motion state with fn_800D769C(fp, ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirFire), calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with Ft_MF_None and ftCo_804D9030/ftCo_804D9034 float constants, clamps air drift, stores arg1 into fp->mv.co.common.x0, and installs accessory4_cb/take_dmg_cb.
Both new functions were wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop blocks, matching the surrounding file's matching-control style.
smart_moves: Used the already-matched nearby ground counterparts as templates: fn_800D7C60 follows fn_800D7BDC, and fn_800D81D0 follows fn_800D8140 with air motion-state IDs.

Preserved exact code-shape details needed for matching, including temporary gobj variables, explicit float loads via extern int symbols cast to f32 pointers, and a scoped HSD_GObj* alias in fn_800D81D0.
Placed ftCommon_ClampAirDrift immediately after Fighter_ChangeMotionState in both air enter functions, which the PR context calls out as the shared air-specific behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in fn_800D81D0, indicating stack-layout sensitivity was handled rather than simplified away.
decomp_lessons: For air variants of already-understood fighter action-state enter functions, start from the ground counterpart and look for the minimal air-specific differences: air motion-state enum plus ftCommon_ClampAirDrift after motion-state change.

Do not remove seemingly redundant temporaries or casts during matching. In this PR, temp_gobj, gobj2, extern int float constants cast through f32*, and PAD_STACK(8) are all likely codegen-shaping artifacts.
Callback setup is part of the action-state enter contract: both matched functions assign accessory4_cb and take_dmg_cb after motion-state setup and common-data initialization.
When constants are represented as data symbols such as ftCo_804D9028, ftCo_804D902C, ftCo_804D9030, and ftCo_804D9034, preserving the original load pattern can be necessary for a match.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-style names such as fn_800D7C60 and fn_800D81D0.
Motion-state IDs use ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirRapid and ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirFire for the air scope rapid/fire states.
Fighter common callbacks are assigned through fields named accessory4_cb and take_dmg_cb.
The file path and symbols use the ftCo/ftCommon split: ftCo_Attack100.c contains common fighter action-state code, while ftCommon_ClampAirDrift provides shared air movement handling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma dont_inline around each new function to prevent inlining from disturbing expected codegen.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in fn_800D81D0 to force the expected stack frame shape.
Introduced temporary object aliases, including Fighter_GObj* temp_gobj and scoped HSD_GObj* gobj2, to match register/type/codegen behavior around Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Loaded float arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState through extern int symbols cast to f32 pointers rather than using literal float constants.
Kept pointer arithmetic for common data access: *(s32*)((u8*)p_ftCommonData + 0x5BC).
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human-text evidence states that both functions match the same pattern as their ground counterparts and call ftCommon_ClampAirDrift after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftCo_Attack100.c air item-scope functions that mirror ground counterparts and may need ftCommon_ClampAirDrift after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Search for uses of ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirRapid and ftCo_MS_ItemScopeAirFire to identify remaining unnamed scope-related callbacks.
Compare fn_800D7C60 against fn_800D7BDC and fn_800D81D0 against fn_800D8140 to validate naming opportunities once surrounding symbols are understood.
Search for PAD_STACK in ftCommon action-state enter functions to identify similar stack-sensitive matching patterns.
